name: Stuffed Pickled Peppers
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Grated fresh or prepared horseradish
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash all vegetables thoroughly before chopping; drain peppers.
2. Cut off tops and save stems; remove seeds and membranes.
3. Cover the tops and peppers with water and bring to a boil. Boil gently for 10 minutes, or until almost tender. Drain and let cool.
4. Combine chopped vegetables in a bowl.
5. Mix remaining ingredients (salt, brown sugar, cider vinegar, cayenne, paprika, dry mustard) separately.
6. Pour the liquid mixture over the chopped vegetables and mix well to combine.
7. Stuff the peppers with the vegetable mixture; do not pack too tightly. Place the tops on each pepper.
8. Tie in place with string.
9. Place the peppers in a wide-mouthed jar or a large crock or jar.
10. In an agate or enamel kettle, heat together all ingredients except olive oil (3 pints cider vinegar, 1 quart water, celery seeds, mustard seeds, whole cloves, whole allspice, and cinnamon).
11. Bring the spice mixture to a boil; pour over the stuffed peppers.
12. Let cool. To each 3 peppers in the jar or crock, add about 1/2 cup olive oil. Cover.
13. Let stand in a cool place for 10 days or longer before using.
14. To serve, remove strings and drain the stuffed peppers.
Note: Use remaining oil and spice mixture in salad dressings or marinades.
